name: Video & Streaming Infringement
description: Infringement scoring overrides for Video & Streaming patents. Covers video codecs, streaming protocols, DRM, and media processing.
scoringGuidance: Video codec patents are frequently standards-essential (H.264/AVC, H.265/HEVC, H.266/VVC, AV1). Any product supporting these codecs necessarily implements patented techniques.
Streaming products implementing DASH, HLS, or CMAF necessarily implement adaptive bitrate, segmentation, and manifest-based delivery.
DRM patents apply to any product implementing Widevine, FairPlay, or PlayReady — the underlying mechanisms are well-documented.
Video processing patents (scaling, deinterlacing, noise reduction) apply to any display or playback product.
necessaryImplicationGuidance: Any H.264/H.265 decoder necessarily implements: (1) entropy decoding, (2) inverse transform, (3) motion compensation, (4) deblocking filter, (5) reference picture management. Any adaptive streaming client necessarily implements: (1) manifest parsing, (2) segment fetching, (3) buffer management, (4) bandwidth estimation, (5) quality switching logic.

questionOverrides.reasoningPrompt: Video codec patents are often standards-essential. Identify if the patent covers techniques required by H.264, H.265, H.266, VP9, AV1, DASH, or HLS specifications.
